title: Active Readiness
subtitle: Staying alert and prepared
description: At this stage, your body may be preparing actively for labor, and signs may appear at any time. Staying alert, minimizing strain, and being ready to respond quickly can help ensure safety and comfort.
what_to_expect: Possible signs of labor such as tightening, discomfort, or pressure
Need for immediate readiness and response
daily_safety_tips: Keep essential items, documents, and contacts ready
Stay close to accessible care and support systems
Avoid unnecessary physical activity or strain
movement_guidelines: Limit movement to essential and gentle activity only
Avoid any activity that causes discomfort or fatigue
Use assistance or support for all movement
environmental_awareness: Ensure surroundings are calm, safe, and easily accessible
Keep pathways clear for quick and safe movement
Continue avoiding exposure to harmful chemicals or smoke
travel_safety: Stay within close reach of healthcare facilities
Ensure maximum comfort and support if travel is unavoidable
important_note: Being alert and ready to respond to body signals is essential at this stage.
reassurance: With awareness and preparation, you can feel ready and supported.
medical_disclaimer: Consult a qualified healthcare professional before starting or modifying any physical activity.

diabetes.personalized_description: Maintaining consistent meals and minimal activity supports stable blood sugar levels during this phase.
movement_guidelines: Engage in minimal gentle movement when comfortable
Maintain consistent meal timing and hydration
Monitor your body's response to reduced activity
diabetes.safety_focus: Watch for fatigue, dizziness, or changes in energy levels.
diabetes.reassurance: Balanced routines support stability and comfort.

anemia.personalized_description: Fatigue may be high, requiring maximum rest and minimal exertion.
movement_guidelines: Limit activity strictly to essential tasks
Take frequent and extended rest periods
anemia.safety_focus: Be aware of dizziness, weakness, or breathlessness.
anemia.reassurance: Rest and minimal effort help maintain comfort.

hypertension.personalized_description: Maintaining a calm, low-stress environment supports stable blood pressure.
movement_guidelines: Avoid stressful or physically demanding situations
Engage in minimal and controlled movement
hypertension.safety_focus: Be mindful of stress levels and physical strain.
hypertension.reassurance: A calm and steady approach supports well-being.

thyroid.personalized_description: Consistent routines and minimal activity support metabolic balance during final stages.
movement_guidelines: Maintain a steady and minimal activity pattern
thyroid.safety_focus: Be aware of unusual fatigue or energy fluctuations.

multi_condition_blend.combined_message: When managing multiple conditions, maintaining readiness, minimizing movement, and prioritizing rest is essential.
priority_guidelines: Avoid unnecessary physical effort or strain
Maintain minimal and essential movement only
Take frequent rest breaks and stay hydrated
multi_condition_blend.reassurance: Careful rest and awareness support comfort and safety.
